provisions.title: Pillar 3 Disclosure Framework Overview
provisions.text: Pillar 3 complements the minimum capital requirements (Pillar 1) and the supervisory review process (Pillar 2) by seeking to promote market discipline through regulatory disclosure requirements. Banks must disclose information on the scope of application, capital, risk exposures, risk assessment processes, and capital adequacy. Disclosures must be made at least semi-annually, with certain disclosures required quarterly for large internationally active banks.

provisions.title: Composition of Capital Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose the full composition of regulatory capital, including: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital and its components; Additional Tier 1 (AT1) capital instruments and their terms; Tier 2 capital instruments; regulatory adjustments and deductions; and a full reconciliation of regulatory capital to the audited balance sheet. The reconciliation must allow market participants to identify all balance sheet items that are used in the calculation of regulatory capital.

provisions.title: Leverage Ratio Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose a detailed breakdown of the leverage ratio, including: the numerator (Tier 1 capital) and denominator (total exposure measure); on-balance sheet exposures (excluding derivatives and SFTs); derivative exposures (replacement cost and potential future exposure); securities financing transaction exposures; off-balance sheet items; and a reconciliation of the total exposure measure to the balance sheet.

provisions.title: Liquidity Disclosure: LCR and NSFR
provisions.text: Banks must disclose quantitative information on the Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR). LCR disclosure includes: total HQLA, total net cash outflows, and the LCR ratio, with a breakdown by significant currency. NSFR disclosure includes: available stable funding (ASF) by maturity bucket and category, required stable funding (RSF) by maturity bucket and asset category, and the NSFR ratio.

provisions.title: Credit Risk Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose detailed information on credit risk exposures. For the standardised approach: exposures by asset class and risk weight, including the effect of credit risk mitigation. For the IRB approach: PD, LGD, EAD, and risk weight by exposure class; a comparison of actual outcomes with previous estimates (backtesting); and details of the models used. All banks must disclose past-due and impaired exposures, provisions, and write-offs by geography, industry, and counterparty type.

provisions.title: Market Risk Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose their market risk capital requirements under each approach used (standardised or IMA). For the standardised approach (FRTB): sensitivities-based method risk charges by risk class, default risk charge, and residual risk add-on. For the IMA: value-at-risk, stressed value-at-risk, incremental risk charge, and comprehensive risk measure. Banks must also disclose the scope and coverage of their trading book, and information on backtesting results.

provisions.title: Operational Risk Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose operational risk capital requirements calculated under the standardised measurement approach (SMA). Disclosures include: the Business Indicator (BI) and its three sub-components (ILDC, SC, FC); the Business Indicator Component (BIC); the Internal Loss Multiplier (ILM) where applicable; the Loss Component; and total operational risk capital requirements. Banks must also disclose the number and aggregate amount of operational risk losses above specified reporting thresholds.

provisions.title: Remuneration Disclosure
provisions.text: Internationally active banks must disclose their remuneration policies and practices. Disclosures include: the composition and mandate of the remuneration committee; the design and structure of the remuneration process; information linking remuneration to risk; the mix of fixed and variable remuneration; the use of deferral arrangements and performance adjustment mechanisms (malus and clawback); and aggregate quantitative information on remuneration split by business area and senior staff.

provisions.title: Counterparty Credit Risk Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks must disclose detailed information on counterparty credit risk exposures. This includes: exposure at default (EAD) by approach (SA-CCR or internal model method); a breakdown of exposures by counterparty type (financial institutions, corporates, sovereigns); credit risk mitigation techniques (netting, collateral, CCP exposures); credit valuation adjustment (CVA) capital charges; and the notional amount and fair value of credit derivative positions used for intermediation and portfolio management.

provisions.title: Securitisation Disclosure
provisions.text: Banks involved in securitisation must disclose: the nature and extent of their involvement (originator, sponsor, investor); the accounting policies and valuation methodologies applied; details of securitisation exposures by role, risk weight band, and approach used; retained or purchased securitisation exposures that are deducted from capital; exposures securitised and recognised gains or losses on sales; and information on the performance of securitised assets.

provisions.title: G-SIB Indicators Disclosure
provisions.text: Global systemically important banks (G-SIBs) must publish a set of 12 indicators used to assess systemic importance. These are grouped into five categories: size (total exposures); interconnectedness (intra-financial system assets, intra-financial system liabilities, securities outstanding); substitutability/financial institution infrastructure (assets under custody, payment activity, underwriting activity); complexity (notional amount of OTC derivatives, Level 3 assets, trading and AFS securities); and cross-jurisdictional activity (cross-jurisdictional claims, cross-jurisdictional liabilities).

provisions.title: TLAC/MREL Disclosure
provisions.text: G-SIBs must disclose information on their total loss-absorbing capacity (TLAC). Disclosures include: the composition of TLAC by instrument type; the TLAC ratio as a percentage of risk-weighted assets and leverage ratio exposure; the maturity profile of TLAC-eligible instruments; subordination of TLAC instruments; issuance entity (resolution entity or subsidiary); and any regulatory deductions applied. This information enables market participants to assess the bank's loss-absorbing and recapitalisation capacity in resolution.

provisions.title: Climate-Related Financial Risk Disclosure
provisions.text: The BCBS has finalised a Pillar 3 disclosure framework for climate-related financial risks. Banks must disclose: governance arrangements for climate risk (board oversight, management responsibilities); climate risk strategy and business model; climate risk management processes (identification, assessment, monitoring, mitigation); quantitative metrics (credit risk exposures by sector and geography, carbon-related assets, financed emissions); climate scenario analysis (methodologies, scenarios used, key assumptions, results); and transition plan information.
